summary:
The design now resolves both feedback items. Both changes are confined to my declared output (`design.md`).

**Summary:** I revised the design-writer's technical design at `.pipeliner/phases/02-plan/main/design-writer/output/design.md` to address the critic's two minor findings, grounding each in the actual repo source (`app/helpers/status_helper.rb`, `app/models/pipeline.rb`, `app/views/pipelines/*`, `guides/ui-style-guide.md`, and the existing tests). For **F1** (color contradiction: an aborted pipeline would show a gray summary dot but a red `status_badge`, since `STATUS_TONES["aborted"] => :danger`), I made tone a single-sourced value — every summary branch now derives its tone from `StatusHelper::STATUS_TONES`, the same table `status_badge` reads — and reconciled the one real conflict with a single table change, retoning `aborted` from `:danger` to `:muted`; this also encodes the design's R9-vs-R11 distinction in color (red reserved for error stops, gray for a deliberate cancel), and I added the matching `guides/ui-style-guide.md` color-table row and the `status_helper.rb` edit to the file plan plus a tone-consistency assertion to the test plan, all in the same PR per CLAUDE.md. For **F2** (system-test expectation), I verified the repo has no system-test base class (`test/system/` and `ApplicationSystemTestCase` don't exist), replaced the aspirational `test/system/...` row with the spec-writer's actual `test/integration/pipeline_live_status_test.rb`, and added an explicit testing-strategy note marking the request-spec coverage as a pragmatic substitution with a browser system test recorded as a Build follow-up. I also logged both fixes in a new "iteration 2 → 3" feedback-resolution section and a §7 design-decision note.
